But ordering is by craft_id (alpha/numeric), not by 'town built around single craft' — so famous craft-towns (Arita, Kanazawa, Wajima, Echizen, Tokoname) aren't the top entries. Agent has the data to answer well but must do its own ranking. No metadata indicating mono-industry concentration vs cluster.
missing_data: 'Mono-industry town' tag indicating town economically/culturally defined by one craft; tourist-visit info (workshop addresses, demonstration schedules); link to densan-shimei page.
improvement_hint: Enrich items with municipality_count and a 'craft_town_canonical' boolean for famous one-craft places (Arita-yaki / 有田町, Wajima-nuri / 輪島市, etc.); allow ordering by importance/fame rather than craft_id.
_query: Japanese towns built around a single craft industry.

reasoning: 83 hits with strong top content: Tomioka Silk Mill (UNESCO 産業遺産), Nagoya industrial-tourism portal, DMO plans from Gunma, Aizu-Wakamatsu, Kazuno mentioning 産業遺産. Body of Kazuno entry lists 史跡尾去沢鉱山, 小坂鉱山事務所 — actually responsive to 'industrial decline preserved'. But response gives DMO planning documents with administrative jargon rather than a curated visitor list. No mention of Hashima/Gunkanjima, Yubari coal museum, or Meiji Industrial Revolution UNESCO cluster.
missing_data: Hashima/Gunkanjima, Yubari coal museum, Meiji Industrial Revolution sites (Yawata steel works, Mitsubishi Nagasaki dockyard), Ashio/Besshi copper mines; visitor-friendly summaries vs DMO plan text.
improvement_hint: Boost spot/attraction entries over dmo_plan PDFs for visitor-intent queries; surface UNESCO Meiji-industrial cluster as a coherent item; tag '近代化産業遺産' explicitly to enable filtering.
_query: Where can I see Japan's response to industrial decline preserved?
